reviews.review_text: The best free game available on Steam. It doesn't demand a lot considering the system requirements, so it will probably even run on a potato pc. In this game, teamwork is the most important element, especially if you are new and don't know how to handle the zombies or how to finish a map (e.g. where to find the objectives or the extraction zone). Compared to other zombie games, such as L4D/2 or Killing Floor, this one is more slow-paced, yet offers an highly realistic and tactical gameplay. The variety of maps allows the player to choose between an either short map, which can be completed within 5-10 minutes, or a longer one (survival maps in particular) to enjoy the waves of zombie slashing.

NMRiH is one of the best Source mods of all time with a unique realistic approach to Zombie Apocalypse; you will feel isolated and will be punished for the slightest hiccup. Getting bitten once may lead to catching the virus therefore zombification.

[b][✔][/b]
> realism at it's finest (within source engine limitations)
> map design with dynamic objectives & horde survival
> huge weapon variety each with unique animation
> good replay value
> optimization (runs on everything)

[b][✘][/b]
> you get "stuck" sometimes when bumping into players
> rarely zombie hitbox is a bit off

Another masterfully implemented mod of the source engine, No More Room in Hell will give you a lot of fun.
The game uses a more realistical approach on the zombie survival genre while offering two game modes: survival and objective missions.
While the survival missions are quite simple, they are very enjoyable and fun, holding the zombies while you wait for extraction at wave 10.
The objective ones are more broad and offer variation within big maps, and while some are easier than others they are still a good adventure. Another fact is that such maps possess variation between rounds, so you will have another route of objectives to execute.
Most of the maps also contain secrets and special features.
You are not a superhero: you will bleed, you will be infected and you can also suicide yourself for the greater good of the group.

Overall review:

Pros:

- Free game.
- Extremely fun with friends (the more people you play, the better).
- Realistic gun mechanics and decent melee combat while keeping a good gameplay.
- Nice graphics (taking in account the fact that the game was made in 2011).
- Extremely well made animations of itens and weapons in first person.
- Very good optimization (very few bugs, and no performance issues at all).
- Good gun and attack sounds.
- Characters possess a varied soundbank of quick chat commands, giving you quick communication with your fellow survivors.
- The VOIP inside the game simulates real conversation, the far you are from another player, the lower is your voice (you can use an in game walkie talkie to communicate from afar without issues).
- Lots of maps with good level design.
- Two map types with lot of variation.
- Very good amount of guns and melee weapons (arround 20 firearms and a bow, and 20 melee weapons, there are also 3 throwable explosives).
- People still play this game to this day (so you will find people to play with you without a problem).

Cons:

- Few zombie models (you will kill the same zombies all over again).
- Some animations of ammo checking are wrong.
- Playing alone is difficult in most cases (not recommended to play alone, this is a cooperative game after all).
- Sometimes the zombie attack animation can be quite poor (depending on you position).
- The music is quite boring (my personal opinion, maybe you will like it).

While the game has minor flaws, it is a very enjoyable experience and will bring you and your friends lots of desperating moments and agony (the good kind of it).
A good adventure.

Overall score:

91/100

A very well done realistic zombie survival game with realistic combat, to fun custom and default maps and community made modes and default modes this game is sure to make you want to play zombie games a lot more.

Awesome game. One of bests free games on Steam.
You can play chain objectives maps or defensive maps (where you can make barricades).

Teamwork is the key here.

[i]Bash the heads of zombies and occasionally your teammates with fists or a sledgehammer. Shoot zombies and sometimes your teammates in the head! Instead of hoarding, why not share all that ammo you're carrying, because you're not really going to use 5.56 with 9mm pistols? Don't forget to bring a friend![/i]

No More Room in Hell is a neat little HL2 mod, that i'd honestly recommend over other zombie games for many, many reasons, such as having an unique atmosphere that really takes you into the game on a perspective where you'll have to work as a team instead of just being a mindless shooter that literally anyone could play.

That being said, NMRiH takes a more realistic approach on the zombie scheme; slow paced action, everything can be used to save your or someone elses ass, ammo is (fairly often) scarce and should be saved rather than just used on the first enemy on sight. It may not be as action driven as one may think but the slow paced gameplay fits really well to the impression of the apocalyptic world, where everyone is trying to survive till they get to safety.

So what about the techinal thingies?

Yes it has some bugs but most of the time you'll barely notice these minor things. Yes there could be some further fixing with the game but overall but in general the game works and functions really well.

It's a really good addition to anyones zombie game collection, no need to wait for sales or anything because it's for grabs at anytime for free!

10/10 Would bash my teammates heads in with fists again and again till i get kicked/banned

I really wanted to enjoy this game, I really did. 
But oh boy, this game is just.. Awful. I'm thankful for it being free to be honest. 

I do see potential in this game, I really do. So, hopefully when maybe it's updated some more the movement of the undead and the combat can be improved. I can't count how many times I bashed one of them in the head and somehow it was still alive, and why couldn't I use an axe to break the wooden pannel across the door? I don't understand, I was at that for ages, all different angles, it just refused to work. 

The one thing that slightly saved this was the soundtracks, and the at least up do date graphics, though that part of a video game isn't something that bothers me. Graphics mean nothing without story.

When I first saw this, I expected a crappy ripoff, However, I was highly surprised when I tried it, Hard as it may be, It's very fun if you do play with your friends, Which is what I did , However , there's a distinct lack of players, But , can be very enjoyable with friends, With very challenging maps. I thoroughly enjoyed it. 7.5/10

Chances are like most of us you probably don't have that skill so just take it easy and enjoy it for what it is.

I've played this game for a long time and every bit was enjoyable. 

Pros -
1. Two modes - Survival and Objective completion
2. Good amount of player base and many servers to choose from
3. Gameplay will get you hooked if you're into zombie objective completion type games.
4. Many weapons to choose from
6. Players can get infected and turn into zombies
7. Some maps are really challenging.
8. In obective maps, you can respawn maximum 2 times( some have 3 ) at various stages of map. This may be a con to some but I like this.

Cons -
1. Some rare zombie and player getting stuck somewhere
2. Server music keeps on playing and I don't know how to stop it. Just like CS 1.6 if you have played it. It plays even after you've left the server
3. Some guns are weird to shoot and won't hit the target like in other games. Especially, the sniper rifles.


It is better to play this game in team or you get killed very early in the round start. However, if you have enough practice, it becomes easy to be a lone wolf. And like L4D, this game would be different everytime you play it. Highly recommended!

A really good blend of classic zombie doomsday situations with co-op gameplay that emulates the dynamics of a group of strangers attempting to work together in a crisis (i.e. people can be a bit of a ♥♥♥♥). Being free, and easy to run, makes this essential for all PC-owning zombie fans.

I love this game but I have to say, the negative reveiws are a little lacking in context. I mean the only reason I got this game was because of a reveiw that talked about how terrible the games mechanics. The OP had 0.1 hours on it, so I had to check it out.

[h1] [b] MY REVIEW [/b] [/h1] 

[b] PROS [/b]
This game has taken zombie survival into a new sort of way making it harder and the environment in the game is creepy and scary, I really enjoy playing this with friends and the ammo is so scarce that to a point that you fight with your bare hands or melee. I suggest this game even though its a few years old coming from the Half-Life 2 engine.

[b] CONS [/b]
There  are some missing 'features' but I can tolerate it since this game was developed as a mod for HL2, Those that are lacking are like proning, broken animations, more missions, probably a capability for a player to become a zombie like in Contagion. Hmmm, also different categories of zombies like for example, runner, raging zombie or a aggressive ghoul which is very fast. Plus we need more variety of weapons.

[b] OVERALL [/b]
This game is very unique in a way that people will love and enjoy playing over and over again, The replayability is endless especially with more friends in tthe picture. This game deservers a remake although I heard that they (Developers of NMRIH) are making NMRIH 2 running in Unreal Engine instead of Source Engine, That'll be cool since Unreal has nice graphics and features. I hope when they release NMRIH 2 I suggest that it will be purchasable since the developers created a amazing game same as the old one but even better!

[quote=author] [b] Reviewed by Wahlols [/b]	[/quote]

[h1] Fun Mod/Game [/h1]

[b] No More Room in Hell is a co-op first person shooter taken place in a zombie filled apocalypse.
The mod or "game" can be challenging to new players as it's extremely hardcore. [/b]

No More Room in Hell is a fun and challenging [u] free to play [/u] "game" on steam consisting of relatively large amounts of content as a mod. The overall gameplay is perfected, but it holds a few bugs that could be easily fixed.
Even though this game turned 6 years old, it still feels like a brand new game when I come back to it.
I would recommend this to any hardcore first person shooter fanboy or that one guy in his mid thirties that watches The Walking Dead.

really nice first person schooter/melee combat game very realistic and very difficult 
i love but it can get a bit boring it's just difficult to get in a nice lobby i guess with some friends this is amazing
i also like the low-budget setting

I downloaded this game a few years back. I saw Cry and Pewds play it back int he day and I decided I'd get it when I could. I finally did. I mostly played with my brother but after that, after having put nine hours into the game , I dropped it because it became boring to me.

The game felt very unfinished, as if it hadn't actually left greenlight. Setting up servers proved to be a hassle, and I found you could only really play with friends who weren't local, was to play on a pre-existing server. Either I was doing something wrong, or this game's Gui wasn't properly implimented. Again, very unfinished and rushed. I'm hoping the sequel will be done better, that is assuming they're hoping to improve upon what they did last time.

That being said, the game was fun at the time. I fondly recall shooting myself without any rhyme or reason, leaving my brother to fend for himself, and, in cases I found most humorous, fight off a hord that has surrounded him with no hope of escape.

It's a game of survival, and survivability is pretty low with a two man squad so bring mate's for a good time. You'll need to work together, it's very unforgiving. Limited weapons, limited ammo, mostly melee weapons, aim for the head, classic Romero zombies.

To clarify, I haven't played enough of it to make a big enough review, this is just my initial impressions from the nine hours I gathered, I haven't even touched the game in over two years so if they've made improvements to the Gui, which I doubt, I wouldn't know.

My hopes for the sequel, is that they focus more on developing the server setup, map selection, gameplay. The realistic approach they've taken is perfect, what I mean is, character customization, that seems to be very lacking in this game, if not implimented badly from my experience. Better maps would be nice too. And a Steam workshop if at all possible. I wouldn't mind downloading a Raccoon City based map and playing with some buds with that.

I fully trust the Dev team and understand if they can't focus real big like that. We'll have to see but that's half my hopes for the sequel, which I pray will improve over this game.

I stand on neutural ground with this game, but I do approve it, only to people looking for a good co-op Zombie game that is as realistic as it can be.

I wish the developers goodluck with the sequel.

I would have recommended the game, but some factors took the fun away. I'm gonna specify those.
* You can't join a ongoing match until a round or wave ends or full team dies
* Even with stock maps game still downloads some files - which bothered me most
* Maps are too small for running or shooting from great distance.
* Inadequte weapons and very low ammount of ammo sures that you can't survive long.
* Zombies are hard to kill and they're great in number - like pile of gerbage thrown at you.
* Not fun at all.

In this game:
-You are blessed with the phisical atributes of a 4 year old, meaning: next to no stamina for mele weapons and running.
-Maps are long and may variate courses to the finish, what is good, but reaching the end of while blessed with 4 year old atribues is another question.
-The chance that you get infected is VERY HIGH, and when you turn it makes your team very happy to deal with a fast zombie up close.
-The game is aslo blessed with the avrage and advanced trolls, who when given the chace will backstab you/or kill you at the very end of the map cause they simply can. So if you play turn friendly fire off or be prepared for a gunfight at the end.

I'll say that I will continue to try and play this game and asside from what I will critizise I think the gameplay is phnominal, now onto what I don't like, the graphics. What I mean by that is that is runs terribly, I'm not sure how it is optimised but it wont run any higher than 20 frames a second and I have a decent computer/3.2 GHz dual core processor a GTX 750ti and 8GBs of RAM so I should be able to run it.

*PRO's*
Mechanical features such as barricading are interesting and work.
Decent variety of items for use.
Lighting is okay.

*CON's*
You need to shoot zombies in the head or they basically do NOT go down.
You will always have to reload before you can kill a zombie half the time.
Game needs drastic aim and hit box correction. 
The movement of the zombies need improvement.
Graphics are poor and need improvement.
Needs some back story
Characters need improvements.

I really don't know what to think of this game, the game can be fun with servers that give you unlimited ammo, having the ability to spawn any gun in the game and with freinds it can be a blast to play but after a while it can get boring, but the playing the game properly it can be a hastle to play, with melee weapons feeling really weak besides the chainsaw and zombies being overpowered at times. On the one hand (for me anyways) No More Room In Hell is a lot of fun to play, but on the other hand it is a hastle to try and play properly. I'd say at least give it a go. (Final score 5.5/10)

i've only played about 8 hours of this so far and i have to say that it is one of the most unintentionally scary games i have ever played. also a pretty damn good co-op shooter

I found attempting this one to be pretty tedious. It took quite a few tries for me and a friend to just get in a game alone together, and then we found that the number of zombies did not seem to be balanced for the number of players. We found it difficult to even escape the room in which we spawned. The very slow attack speed and substantial health of the zombies did not help matters. We've played many games of this type (L4D1 and 2, Contagion), and this is the worst.

i like this game quite well but the reason i gave this a thumbs down is beacause of the graphics i have a decent gaming computer and they are still quite bad and there are some bad glitches like zombies spawning inside the safe house when you start a map but on the plus side it has really good hand physics and ill recommend it for that

I cant believe back in 2013 I could not give this fantastic game a review.
Now, I do not remember much from my gameplay experience as it was back 4 years ago, wow, time flies, Hello 2017.
This game was made a whopping 6 years ago but still is considered one of the best zombie Free-To-Play games of all time.

Pros:
Graphics are good for a 6 year old game
Fun
Simple
Great community
Zombies!!!!!

Cons:
I have not seen many flaws in this game thus far :)
 
Recommended if die hard fan or not of zombie games

I cannot understand why this game is so overrated.

Graphics are ok, maybe the only that we can't criticize is that the game is pretty nicely optimized. You don't need a good machine to play it. Alright so now let's see why I don't recommend this game, first of all this game is just a L4D in a very very bad version, zombies are overpowered, weapons are like doing the same damage as I was throwing a snowball on a zombie. I didn't play that much [i](~1 hour idk why steam not showing it)[/i] but the community was literally cancerous, most of the maps were ♥♥♥♥. Plus you get bored really quickly, it could maybe let you have fun for one afternoon or two but otherwise you will quickly unistall it I think this game can only be fun with a mate and for a short time and even a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ dead worm is 10x faster and has more stamina than you.
